name: a low-confidence OCR-derived health state must not auto-supersede a confident prior
description: docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Guards': facts derived from OCR carry reduced confidence, and low-confidence numeric health facts never auto-supersede anything. Note 1 records a confident medication regimen (lisinopril, conf 0.95, typed by the owner). Note 2 is an OCR read of a blurry pharmacy label that the model returns as a contradicting regimen at conf 0.25. Spec-correct behaviour: the low-confidence OCR fact does NOT auto-supersede; the confident fact stays active and a review (low_confidence / fact_conflict) is filed for a human. Today supersession.decide() takes NO confidence and the pipeline files no low_confidence guard, so the 0.25 OCR fact eagerly supersedes the 0.95 one — a silent overwrite by garbage. xfail until the confidence guard is implemented (red-team H2: guards unimplemented).
xfail: Health confidence guard unimplemented (red-team H2) — supersession.decide() takes no confidence and the pipeline never short-circuits a low-confidence health supersession, so the OCR fact (conf 0.25) supersedes the confident prior. Currently fails: "expected a fact matching {'entity': 'Me', 'predicate': 'medicationRegimen', 'qualifier': 'antihypertensive', 'value_contains': 'lisinopril', 'status': 'active'}, found none" (lisinopril was superseded) and "expected NO fact matching {... 'value_contains': 'lisinopril', 'status': 'superseded'}, found 1".

steps.body: Confirmed with Dr. Okafor: I'm on lisinopril 10mg daily.
Photo of the pharmacy label (blurry OCR): looks like it might say lisartan 50mg?

facts.statement: Pharmacy label (OCR, low confidence) reads losartan 50mg daily.
